title: Restructure all framework artifacts into the .excn/ namespace
problem_statement: A scaffolded Instance spreads framework files across the host repo's root — four methodology docs, a schemas directory, and tmp/exec work-tracking. Host projects experience this as clutter and collision risk against their own files and their existing agent-instruction surfaces. Research confirmed the deeper issue: nothing about the root spread is needed for agents to find the docs, because no harness ingests folders without a pointer — while root files ARE swept into default search and editor indexes, violating the principle that agents get only what they need when they need it.
solution: Move everything the framework owns into one root dotfolder, .excn/ (ADR-0002): the four methodology docs, schemas, ADRs, durable research, and ephemeral work-tracking. The host root keeps exactly one touch — a small explicit-path plain-text pointer block in its existing agent-instruction file, appended non-destructively. Ships as to-execution 0.2.0; the root Instance migrates by hand.

user_stories.actor: Team Lead setting up a new project
user_stories.want: the framework to land in a single dotfolder
user_stories.benefit: the host root stays clean and deleting .excn/ removes the framework entirely

user_stories.want: the scaffolder to never overwrite, rename, or bloat my existing agent-instruction files
user_stories.benefit: my tools' live instruction surfaces keep working (research assumption A)

user_stories.want: a root pointer with full explicit paths into .excn/
user_stories.benefit: I load framework docs only when I need them — dotfolder content never surfaces by accident

user_stories.want: the pointer block to stay tiny
user_stories.benefit: my 32 KiB instruction chain is not truncated by the framework

user_stories.want: the template and every Instance to share the .excn shape
user_stories.benefit: Src-First mirroring stays a mechanical copy
one namespace owns ephemeral state too, and the CLI guarantees the gitignore

user_stories.want: ADRs and research reports colocated in .excn/
user_stories.benefit: the project's durable memory lives in one place
consumers get the new layout from npx with identity protections intact

implementation_decisions.title: Namespace per ADR-0002
implementation_decisions.summary: .excn/ at the Instance root holds CONTEXT.md, PHILOSOPHY.md, PROCESS.md, TEAM_DIRECTIVE.md, adr/, schemas/, research/, and tmp/ (work-tracking, gitignored). No file inside .excn/ is named CLAUDE.md or AGENTS.md — those filenames trigger per-directory auto-ingestion.

implementation_decisions.title: Template restructure
implementation_decisions.summary: The Scaffolder template adopts the identical .excn layout; tmp/exec/ disappears in favor of .excn/tmp/{sprints,issues,prds,retros}. The template carries NO root instruction file — a template-stamped CLAUDE.md would be skipped by skip-safety on exactly the hosts that already have one, stranding them unwired.

implementation_decisions.title: Pointer wiring
implementation_decisions.summary: A small plain-text block with full explicit paths, CLI-owned (embedded asset, applied outside the manifest walk). Appended non-destructively and idempotently to EVERY existing file among {CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md} — Claude Code reads only the former, Codex/Cursor/Copilot/Devin the latter; if neither exists, BOTH are created as minimal pointer files. The idempotency sentinel is visible text, never an HTML comment (Claude Code strips comments before injection). Pointer files are append-only under every flag including --force. Claude @import is banned (inlines at launch); the CLI warns without failing when a post-append AGENTS.md exceeds Codex's hard 32 KiB chain cap.

implementation_decisions.title: CLI changes
implementation_decisions.summary: init stamps the .excn layout and stamps .excn/.gitignore (containing tmp/) so the framework owns its ignore rule inside its own namespace — the host's root .gitignore is never touched and ensureGitignore() is deleted. Skip-safety and idempotency semantics unchanged for manifest files; pointer append is governed by the wiring decision above.

implementation_decisions.title: Cross-reference sweep
implementation_decisions.summary: Every path reference in the skill, agent definitions, principles, authoring guide, process doc, and README moves to .excn paths — Src-First, then root mirrors.

implementation_decisions.title: Root Instance migrates by hand
implementation_decisions.summary: Root methodology docs move into .excn/, tmp/exec state moves to .excn/tmp/ preserving all sprint, issue, PRD, and progress history. ADR-0001 (personal ownership) is finalized from packager's draft into .excn/adr/.

implementation_decisions.title: EXEC-001 disposition
implementation_decisions.summary: EXEC-001's decision half is resolved by ADR-0002; its implementation half is superseded by this PRD's issue set. EXEC-001 closes citing both.

implementation_decisions.title: Version
implementation_decisions.summary: Lands as 0.2.0 — pre-1.0 layout change, no migration tooling for stamped Instances (the root is the only Instance; it migrates by hand).

testing_decisions: package-qa gates every CLI/template change; its rubric is Team-Lead-authored and updates in the same window as the contract it asserts (slice 1: .excn manifest + nested gitignore; slice 3: pointer-append checks) so no slice is gated on stale rules.

Pointer-append inversion tests: existing host content preserved byte-for-byte with the block appended exactly once; second run appends nothing; --force run also preserves host content byte-for-byte; host file without trailing newline; both files existing; user-deleted block is re-appended; neither file existing produces both minimal pointer files.

Acceptance: npx to-execution@0.2.0 init in a scratch dir is byte-identical to a local stamp; one scratch host with a pre-existing CLAUDE.md and one with a pre-existing AGENTS.md both show non-destructive wiring; 32 KiB post-append warning fires on an oversized AGENTS.md.
Known HITL step at publish: npm 2FA requires Brooks's OTP run or a fresh one-time granular token — planned, not a surprise.
out_of_scope: The skill fork-vs-adapt resolutions (EXEC-006 catalog, EXEC-007/008/009) — EXEC-006 runs against the post-restructure shape.
Per-tool enhancements (path-scoped .claude/rules, .cursor rule files) — pointer block only for 0.2.0.
Migration tooling for third-party Instances stamped at 0.1.0.
notes: Decided by ADR-0002 (.excn/adr/0002-excn-namespace.md), grounded in .excn/research/agent-context-loading.md (six harnesses, fetched 2026-06-07).

Authored natively as schema-validated JSON — first PRD with no markdown stopgap; the to-prd friction list in PRD-001 notes applies to this run too (tracker/label assumptions), logged as further EXEC-008 evidence.

Amended 2026-06-07 after packager review: nested .excn/.gitignore replaces host-root gitignore append (R1); dual-file pointer coverage CLAUDE.md+AGENTS.md (R2); visible-text sentinel (R3); pointer files force-exempt (R4); 32KiB warn (R5); pointer block CLI-owned, never template-stamped (M1); package-qa rubric co-updates (M2). All accepted by Team Lead.
